quoteFirst: Even in the extreme West of England the difference between the two only amounted to twenty-three minutes, and it was found that no practical inconvenience resulted from saying that the sun rose at
quoteLast: on March 22, rather than at six o'clock.
title: The Royal Observatory, Greenwich: A Glance at Its History and Work
author: Maunder, E. Walter (Edward Walter), 1851-1928

title: The Alps
author: Lunn, Arnold, 1888-1974
